text: The sun dipped below the horizon, painting the sky in hues of amber and rose. I sat on the porch, coffee in hand, watching the neighborhood slowly go quiet. A dog barked somewhere in the distance. Then nothing. Just the soft creak of the porch swing and the distant hum of a highway that never sleeps. It reminded me of summers at my grandmothers place, where the evenings stretched out like taffy and the fireflies blinked their secret code against the darkening trees. She would sit beside me without saying a word. She knew when words were just noise, when silence said more than any sentence could.
